text.title: user-story-mapping Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for user-story-mapping as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Capturing non-functional requirements (performance, security, accessibility, audit) as story map cells.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: NFRs have no natural cell on the map and are routinely dropped in the vertical-slice mentality. Capture them in a parallel NFR register linked to the map header; review per release slice, not per cell.

failure-mode.description: Treating a single-axis prioritized backlog as a story map.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A list with no horizontal journey axis loses the context that explains why stories exist; BAs cannot trace them to process steps or write a coherent RTM.

failure-mode.description: Flat backlog — stories written without journey context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No horizontal axis means no way to detect missing tasks or trace requirements to process steps.

failure-mode.description: Technical stories on the map ("As a system, I want to batch-process orders").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: INVEST requires user perspective; technical stories belong in a separate architectural backlog, not the story map.

failure-mode.description: No release lines defined — map becomes a prioritized list.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without horizontal slice boundaries, the team has no commitment to what ships together and cannot plan iterations.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Workshops optimize the happy path; add a red-sticky row for exception paths below each task band so they are visible, not forgotten.
